```md
---
applyTo: "**/*.tsx,**/*.ts,**/*.css"
---

# Design Guidelines

## Core Principles
- Prioritize clarity, simplicity, and usability.
- Design for users to complete tasks with minimal cognitive effort.
- Keep interfaces clean and free of unnecessary visual elements.
- Maintain a consistent experience across all pages and components.

## Layout & Structure
- Use a consistent page layout and predictable navigation.
- Align elements to a shared layout grid.
- Group related content together and separate unrelated sections with whitespace.
- Avoid clutter by limiting the number of competing visual elements on a screen.
- Design responsively to provide a seamless experience across screen sizes.

## Spacing System
- Use a consistent spacing scale throughout the application.
- Maintain uniform spacing between similar elements.
- Increase spacing to indicate separation between distinct sections.
- Ensure adequate padding inside interactive components.
- Avoid arbitrary spacing values.

## Typography
- Establish a clear visual hierarchy using size, weight, and spacing.
- Keep font choices consistent across the application.
- Prioritize readability with appropriate line height and paragraph spacing.
- Limit the number of typography styles used on a single screen.
- Avoid excessive capitalization and decorative text.

## Color Usage
- Use color intentionally to communicate meaning and hierarchy.
- Maintain sufficient contrast between text and backgrounds.
- Reserve accent colors for primary actions and important states.
- Use semantic colors consistently for success, warning, error, and informational feedback.
- Never rely solely on color to convey information.

## Components
- Reuse existing components before creating new ones.
- Keep component appearance and behavior consistent throughout the application.
- Ensure interactive elements have clearly defined default, hover, focus, active, and disabled states.
- Use consistent iconography, border radius, elevation, and sizing.
- Favor composition over one-off component variants.

## Accessibility
- Design with keyboard navigation in mind.
- Ensure all interactive elements have visible focus indicators.
- Provide descriptive labels for controls and form fields.
- Support screen readers with meaningful structure and semantics.
- Avoid interactions that depend solely on hover.
- Ensure touch targets are comfortably sized for mobile devices.

## Forms & Feedback
- Keep forms simple and logically organized.
- Clearly distinguish required and optional fields.
- Validate input early and provide actionable error messages.
- Confirm successful actions with clear feedback.
- Prevent accidental destructive actions through confirmation where appropriate.

## Content & Usability
- Use concise, user-focused language.
- Prefer recognition over recall by making options visible.
- Keep primary actions prominent and secondary actions unobtrusive.
- Minimize the number of steps required to complete common tasks.
- Handle empty, loading, and error states gracefully.

## Visual Consistency
- Maintain consistent alignment, spacing, and sizing throughout the application.
- Avoid unnecessary animations; use motion only to improve understanding.
- Keep visual styling predictable and cohesive.
- Regularly review designs for consistency before introducing new patterns.
```
